verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, a group of sperm/germline immunoglobulin-domain proteins involved in gamete fusion. IZUMO1 is a well-characterized sperm surface protein essential for sperm-egg fusion; IZUMO4 has a signal peptide and is reported to form complexes with other IZUMO family members on sperm. While the NCBI summary notes nuclear localization, the protein has documented secreted/surface biology in the context of sperm where it associates with IZUMO1-containing complexes. Display is restricted to the germline lineage, making this tissue_restricted_surface rather than yes. Evidence is indirect (via complex partnership) and the nuclear annotation introduces real ambiguity.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Conflict between NCBI nuclear annotation and IZUMO-family sperm-surface complex biology; IZUMO4 lacks TM/GPI itself and may only be a soluble complex partner.
